briefdescription.para: Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.

memberdef.argsstring: (XHdmi_Menu *InstancePtr, u32 UartBaseAddress, u8 *ForceIndependentPtr, u8 *SystemEventPtr, u8 *IsTxPresentPtr, u8 *IsRxPresentPtr, void *ChangeColorbarOutputCB, void *ConfigureTpgEnableInputCB, void *ToggleHdmiRxHpdCB)
memberdef.name: XHdmi_MenuInitialize

briefdescription.para: This function takes care of the HDMI menu initialization.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Menu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is called to trigger the HDMI menu statemachine.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Menu instance.
is the value used for the next menu state decoder.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the menu to the main menu.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Menu instance.

briefdescription.para: This is the main header file for the Xilinx Menu implementation as used in the HDMI example design.

detaileddescription.para: This driver supports Virtual Memory. The RTOS is responsible for calculating the correct device base address in Virtual Memory space.
This driver is not thread safe. Any needs for threads or thread mutual exclusion must be satisfied by the layer above this driver.

Asserts are used within all Xilinx drivers to enforce constraints on argument values. Asserts can be turned off on a system-wide basis by defining at compile time, the NDEBUG identifier. By default, asserts are turned on and it is recommended that users leave asserts on during development.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
X.X .. DD-MM-YYYY .. 1.0 mmo 24-04-2019 Initial version

detaileddescription.para: This driver supports Virtual Memory. The RTOS is responsible for calculating the correct device base address in Virtual Memory space.
This driver is not thread safe. Any needs for threads or thread mutual exclusion must be satisfied by the layer above this driver.

Asserts are used within all Xilinx drivers to enforce constraints on argument values. Asserts can be turned off on a system-wide basis by defining at compile time, the NDEBUG identifier. By default, asserts are turned on and it is recommended that users leave asserts on during development.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
X.X .. DD-MM-YYYY .. 1.00 GM 05/03/18 Initial release.
